purpose: Canonical plan-aware classification of current ChatGPT Plus product capabilities, account-visible surfaces, ELEMENTARY project verification and live runtime activation.

classification_rule.product_available: Documented by a current official OpenAI source for ChatGPT Plus or all ChatGPT plans. This does not prove rollout, account visibility, regional availability, connection, authorization or successful execution.
classification_rule.account_visible: The capability is visible in the owner's current ChatGPT account or current chat tool inventory. Visibility can vary by rollout, platform and region.
classification_rule.project_verified: The capability or governed integration is verified in ELEMENTARY repository source, connected tools or immutable execution evidence.
classification_rule.runtime_active: The capability has current live readback or execution evidence in the applicable surface.

official_sources.title: ChatGPT pricing
official_sources.role: current Plus feature and plan comparison

official_sources.title: ChatGPT release notes
official_sources.role: current rollout, platform and regional caveats

official_sources.title: ChatGPT Work
official_sources.role: Work execution surface and approval behavior

official_sources.title: Developer mode and custom MCP apps
official_sources.role: custom MCP plan boundary and action restrictions

official_sources.title: Deep research in ChatGPT
official_sources.role: research planning, sources, connected-app read boundary and export

capabilities: multi-step work across apps, files, browser and approved context
documents, presentations, spreadsheets, reports and site prototypes
desktop use of local files, apps, browser and Codex
desktop, web, cloud, CLI and IDE coding surfaces subject to actual connection
multiple repository workflows and pull-request review
computer and browser use where available
isolated worktrees, subagents and structured execution
maximum supported frequency once per hour
approved read and selected action capabilities
apps connecting to external and internal tools when supported
connected-app research with read-only app actions
citations, activity history and report export

required_behavior: use the strongest account-visible capability when it reduces manual steps without weakening governance
prefer Work for multi-stage deliverables and cross-app synthesis when visible
prefer scheduled tasks for recurring monitoring and meaningful-change alerts that do not require ADCP lifecycle authority
prefer Codex for bounded repository coding under isolated branch/worktree and pull-request rules
use plugins and apps only with least-privilege permission and explicit confirmation for external writes
use deep research for evidence acquisition and cited synthesis, not authoritative mutation
use projects and memory for continuity and convenience, never as canonical project truth
register durable outputs and execution evidence in the canonical repository
retain browser, GitHub OIDC, Node and manual governed fallbacks for product or rollout unavailability

drift_writeback.plan_or_product_change: Update this profile from current official OpenAI sources.
drift_writeback.account_visibility_change: Record account-visible evidence without exposing private content or credentials.
drift_writeback.project_integration_change: Update owner authorization, capability bootstrap and applicable integration contracts.
drift_writeback.runtime_change: Update the canonical runtime inventory or a dedicated runtime evidence record.
drift_writeback.durable_output: Register through the result/artifact writeback policy.
